title: Mana Crystals
summary: Learn variables by storing magic words and numbers in mana crystals.
learningGoals: Understand that a variable is a name that remembers a value.
Store words and numbers with one equals sign.
Use a variable name later to print the value it remembers.

tutorial.title: Meet the Mana Crystal
tutorial.narration: A mana crystal can remember one piece of magic for you. In Python, that kind of memory is called a variable.
tutorial.callout: The name is on the left. The remembered value is on the right.

tutorial.title: Store a Spell
tutorial.narration: The equals sign tells Python to store the value in the name. Read it as: crystal remembers Glow.
tutorial.callout: Words need quotes so Python knows they are words.

tutorial.title: Say What It Remembers
tutorial.narration: After a crystal remembers a value, use the crystal name to get that value back.
tutorial.callout: Do not put quotes around the variable name when you want its stored value.

spellbookPages.title: Variables Are Mana Crystals
spellbookPages.body: A variable is a name that remembers a value. In this chapter, every variable is like a mana crystal with a label.
spellbookPages.remember: Variable names go on the left side of the equals sign.

spellbookPages.title: Word Magic Needs Quotes
spellbookPages.body: When you store a word, put the word inside quotes. The quotes tell Python the value is text.
spellbookPages.remember: Words use quotes. Variable names do not.

spellbookPages.title: Numbers Fit Too
spellbookPages.body: A variable can remember a number. Numbers do not need quotes.
spellbookPages.remember: Use quotes for words, but not for numbers.

spellbookPages.title: Call the Crystal Name
spellbookPages.body: Use the variable name when you want Python to bring back the value it remembers.
spellbookPages.remember: print(spell) prints the value inside spell.

spells.name: Glow
spells.description: A small word spell that lights up a correct variable.
spells.metaphor: The spell wakes up when a crystal remembers a word.

spells.name: Mana Pop
spells.description: A bright pop of number magic.
spells.metaphor: The spell wakes up when a crystal remembers a number.

baseEffect.successText: Mana Pop bursts for 2 magic damage.

spells.name: Crystal Call
spells.description: A spell that calls back a stored value.
spells.metaphor: The spell wakes up when you use a variable name.

baseEffect.successText: Crystal Call rings for 3 magic damage.

spells.name: Tower Burst
spells.description: A bigger spell made from two variables.
spells.metaphor: The spell wakes up when word magic and number magic work together.

baseEffect.successText: Tower Burst lands for 6 fixed boss damage.

battles.title: The Glimmer Gate

enemy.name: Dim Rune
enemy.description: A sleepy rune blocks the hallway until it sees a few steady word spells.

intent.text: Dim Rune attacks for 1. Missed chants make it zap for 2.

battles.setupText: The first gate needs a few word sparks. Fill the blank so the crystal remembers Glow. Dim Rune attacks after each turn, and missed chants make it hit harder.
battles.victoryText: The rune glows bright, gives one last happy blink, and the gate opens.

challenge.title: Store a Word Spell
challenge.prompt: Fill the blank with the word Glow in quotes.
hints: The word Glow needs quotes around it.
Try putting Glow on the right side of the equals sign.
The line should read like: spell remembers Glow.
challenge.validationNotes: Accepted answers are blank-only values for the ___ token.

spellEffect.successText: Correct code casts Glow for 2 fixed damage.

battles.title: The Mana Meter

enemy.name: Empty Meter
enemy.description: A floating meter waits for a number crystal.

battles.setupText: The meter asks for 5 drops of mana. Store the number 5 in the variable.
battles.victoryText: The meter fills with bright blue light.

challenge.title: Store a Number
challenge.prompt: Fill the blank with the number 5.
hints: Numbers do not need quotes.
Put 5 on the right side of the equals sign.
mana = 5 means mana remembers the number 5.
challenge.validationNotes: Accepted answers are blank-only values for the ___ token.

spellEffect.successText: Correct code casts Mana Pop for 2 fixed damage.

battles.title: Mirror Mist

enemy.name: Mirror Mist
enemy.description: A shining mist copies whatever value your crystal remembers.

battles.setupText: The mist needs you to use the variable name, not the word in quotes.
battles.victoryText: The mist shows Spark and fades away.

challenge.title: Use the Crystal Name
challenge.prompt: Fill the blank with the variable name so Python prints Spark.
hints: Use the variable name that is already on the first line.
Do not add quotes around crystal.
print(crystal) asks Python for the value inside crystal.
challenge.validationNotes: Accepted answers are blank-only values for the ___ token.

spellEffect.successText: Correct code casts Crystal Call for 3 fixed damage.

sideQuests.title: Practice Wand Label
sideQuests.summary: Help a young wizard label a practice wand with a word variable.
sideQuests.rewardText: Mira adds a star sticker to your spellbook.

challenge.title: Name the Practice Wand
challenge.prompt: Fill the blank with the word Star in quotes.
hints: Star is a word, so it needs quotes.
Put the quoted word after the equals sign.
The variable wand_name will remember the word.
challenge.validationNotes: Accepted answers are blank-only values for the ___ token.

sideQuests.title: Mana Jar Count
sideQuests.summary: Help count the mana jars before the tower door closes.
sideQuests.rewardText: Keeper Sol gives you a tiny shield charm.

challenge.title: Store the Jar Count
challenge.prompt: Fill the blank with the number 3.
hints: The answer is a number, so it does not need quotes.
Put 3 after the equals sign.
mana_jars = 3 means the variable remembers 3.
challenge.validationNotes: Accepted answers are blank-only values for the ___ token.

spellEffect.successText: Correct code grants 1 fixed shield point.

sideQuests.title: Crystal Echo
sideQuests.summary: Help the tower echo repeat the value stored inside a crystal.
sideQuests.rewardText: The Echo Sprite opens a tiny shortcut to the boss door.

challenge.title: Call Back the Crystal
challenge.prompt: Fill the blank with the variable name so Python prints Moon.
hints: Use the variable name from the first line.
Do not put quotes around crystal_word.
print(crystal_word) asks Python for the value inside the crystal.
challenge.validationNotes: Accepted answers are blank-only values for the ___ token.

bossFight.title: Boss Fight: Locked Tower Core

boss.name: Locked Tower Core
boss.description: A glowing tower lock that opens only when two mana crystals work together.

bossFight.setupText: The tower core asks for a word crystal and a number crystal. Type the guided code to charge both crystals.
bossFight.victoryText: The tower core opens. You have learned the first secret of variables.

challenge.title: Charge Two Mana Crystals
challenge.prompt: Type the code that stores Luna in crystal_name, stores 7 in mana_power, then prints both variables.
hints: First make crystal_name remember the word Luna.
Then make mana_power remember the number 7.
Print each variable name without quotes.
challenge.validationNotes: Accepted answers are full-code solutions. UI may normalize trailing whitespace before comparing.

spellEffect.successText: Correct code casts Tower Burst for 6 fixed boss damage.

bossFight.rewardText: Chapter 1 complete: Variable Apprentice badge unlocked.

adultGuidance: This POC chapter uses gentle fantasy language to teach variables as named memory boxes. Correct answers trigger fixed JSON spell effects; learner code should never set uncapped damage.
